item.title: Rachel Love Talks About Her Upcoming Reissues, New Band, and Learning from Grief and Loss
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
You can’t leave the house these days without hearing some sloppy “A I” “music,” so having authentic music-makers like Rachel Love (solo, Railcard, Telecom, Dolly Mixture) has never been more urgently important. Her gentle touch when creating gorgeous pop music feels like an antidote to all the ugliness going on in the world. She released …

Continue reading "Rachel Love Talks About Her Upcoming Reissues, New Band, and Learning from Grief and Loss"

item.title: New interview with Would-Be-Goods plus tips for songwriters from Jessica
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Happy release day to WOULD-BE-GOODS!  When Peter Momtchiloff interviewed Jessica Griffin for chickfactor 13 in Y2K, we wonder if he knew he would become partners with Jessica in music and life? Either way, we are happy the Would-Be-Goods restarted as a live and recording pop group in the early 2000s and have played at many …

Continue reading "New interview with Would-Be-Goods plus tips for songwriters from Jessica"

item.title: I WANT IT ALL: my funny valentine gift guide
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
2026 est merde but there are small ways to show your loved ones some love and support your local community, band, label, bookshop, record store, chocolatier, venue, and letterpress genius on this most corporate and silly of holidays. It has never been more important to vote with every dollar you spend, so consider putting it …

Continue reading "I WANT IT ALL: my funny valentine gift guide"

item.title: the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round five
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Kim Baxter – All Girl Summer Fun Band + Rocketship Favorite Music Experiences of 2025 I was super lucky and grateful to be included in some amazing music projects & shows in 2025, including: All Girl Summer Fun Band opening for Bratmobile at Revolution Hall in Portland! Super fun (and hot) summer backyard show with …

Continue reading "the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round five"

item.title: the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round four
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Jed Smith – Jeanines Cindy Lee’s “Diamond Jubilee”. It didn’t come out in 2025 but I don’t think I heard it until earlier this year. It’s a fantastically inspiring piece of work that has lit a renewed creative fire under me. “I Saw the TV Glow” Again, it came out in 2024 but I don’t …

Continue reading "the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round four"

item.title: the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round three
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Stephin Merritt – The Magnetic Fields Movies:  Art Spiegelman: Disaster Is My Muse  Echo Valley  Homemade Gatorade  It Was Just an Accident  The Last Republican  Peaches Goes Bananas  Pee-Wee As Himself  Sanatorium Under the Sign of the Hourglass  Secret Mall Apartment  This Unremarkable Life  Riley Riley (Artsick) favs of 2025: Misc: Joshua Tree National Park …

Continue reading "the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round three"

item.title: the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round two
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Bridget St. John: many things I adore 2025 • my three cats – ever loving ever grateful • the 30+ sparrows who gather beneath my window every morning for black sunflower seeds – ever thankful • recording and then performing with Wilie Aron, Emily Wittbrodt & David Nagler at The Bitter End • the FolkEast …

Continue reading "the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round two"

item.title: the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round one
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Ed Mazzucco & Laura Mazzucco (Shelflife Records, Tears Run Rings, Autocollants) Our new kittens Diego & Mo (pictured above) The Autocollants Reunion Japan vacation Highspire “Crushed” The Cords Quickly Quickly “I Heard That Noise” Stereolab “Instant Holograms On Metal Film” The Blue Herons “Demon Slayer” single Vinyl Williams “Portasymphony” Teenage Daydream book The Umbrellas are …

Continue reading "the chickfactor best of 2025 lists: round one"

item.title: chickfactor 2025 lists: all the things photographer theresa kereakes adored in 2025
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Paul McCartney Photographs 1963-64, Eyes of the Storm – a beautifully curated and deftly installed exhibit with about 250 photographs by Sir Beatle.  The exhibit is as much about photography as it is about a young man making documentary souvenirs of a trip to America where he coincidentally is the center of attention.  Even if …

Continue reading "chickfactor 2025 lists: all the things photographer theresa kereakes adored in 2025"

item.title: chickfactor 2025 lists: rob pursey (skep wax, heavenly, etc.) on his top ten t-shirts
item.dc:creator: gail
item.description:
Rob Pursey (Heavenly, Swansea Sound, the Catenary Wires, Skep Wax Records) on his favorite band T-shirts of 2025 Band T-shirts are pretty much the only clothes I buy these days, apart from when essential items completely wear out and have to be replaced.  There are many advantages to buying band T-shirts, and here are some …

Continue reading "chickfactor 2025 lists: rob pursey (skep wax, heavenly, etc.) on his top ten t-shirts"
